title: Version control and code backup
question: Should the project be kept under version control, and where should the code live?
rationale: Track the project with git (records every change so you can roll back) and back it up to a PRIVATE GitHub repo — kept private because it isn't meant to be shared yet. Trade-off: the shared viewer is symlinked (d35), so a fresh clone needs one generate.py run to rebuild the links.

options.label: Git, pushed to a private GitHub repo

tradeoffs.note: every change is recorded and the code is safe off your machine
private repo — not visible to anyone else while the project is in progress
the viewer is symlinked (d35), so a fresh clone shows dangling links until generate.py recreates them
full change history, but no copy off the machine — a disk loss loses it
anyone can see and clone it
exposes the project publicly before it's ready to share
no change history and no backup — mistakes can't be undone
